rationale: The only resume change swapped three employer names for non-Western firms (Naver, Tencent, MercadoLibre), yet the score stayed identical at 5/10 and both evaluations rest on the same substantive concerns: basic German below the C1 requirement, no UML/IT-architecture leadership, and no team/client management. The wording shifts (e.g., 'Python-adjacent (Node.js)', 'IT consulting context', 'dev-ops infrastructure') are reframings within ordinary run-to-run variation and do not penalize the candidate for the new employer names — the variant never credits or docks the non-Western firms relative to the originals.
